channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TUK Diploma Courses, Fees, and Admission Requirements
item.description: The article provides comprehensive information about the Technical University of Kenya (TUK) diploma programs, including course offerings, tuition fees, and admission criteria. TUK offers over 60 dipl...

item.title: Karachi Rangers Attack Linked to Afghanistan-Based Terrorists, Says Police
item.description: The police have confirmed that the attack on a Rangers camp in Karachi last month was orchestrated with support from Afghanistan. The incident occurred on June 27, 2026, when terrorists targeted the l...

item.title: Legal dispute over trademark names between Indian and US Anthropics intensifies
item.description: A Belagavi-based IT company, Anthropic Software Technology Company (STC), has initiated legal proceedings against its US-based counterpart, Anthropic PBC, over alleged trademark violations. The firm c...

item.title: Military Veterans Urge FG to Implement Approved Salary Adjustments for Armed Forces Personnel
item.description: Retired military officers have called on the Federal Government to implement a ₦250,000 minimum wage for serving armed forces personnel immediately. The veterans claim the government delayed executing...

item.title: Two Homes in Sault Ste. Marie Suffer Extensive Fire Damage
item.description: A fire has caused significant damage to two residential properties in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, according to a report by CTV News. The incident occurred on July 13, 2026, and emergency services were ...

item.title: Accountability for the DA: Facing Scrutiny Like It Demands of Others
item.description: The article critiques the Democratic Alliance (DA) for its inconsistent application of accountability standards. While the DA has long demanded investigations into scandals involving the ANC, EFF, and...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Continues on July 14, 2026
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte entered its fifth day on July 14, 2026, with key developments unfolding. The prosecution called Zuleika Lopez, a close aide to Duterte, to testify ...

item.title: Two Men Arrested After Allegedly Stealing Keys and Stolen Subaru From Mitchell Dealership
item.description: Two men have been arrested after police allege they snatched a set of keys from a Mitchell car dealership and drove off in a stolen Subaru Outback. The incident occurred on Friday, July 10, when the p...

item.title: Wildlife Rehabilitation Centers Face Challenges as University of Saskatchewan Suspends Wildlife Services
item.description: The University of Saskatchewan's decision to indefinitely suspend its wildlife program has raised concerns among wildlife rehabilitation centers and veterinary students. The Western College of Veterin...

item.title: Entrepreneurship in the informal sector: A story of resilience and opportunity
item.description: The article highlights the journey of Tlangelani Pearl Mamba, a 38-year-old woman from Mpumalanga who transitioned from selling homemade atchar on the side to securing a formal job as a car sales exec...

item.title: Tanzania's Strategy for Achieving SDGs Through Vision 2050
item.description: Tanzania has reaffirmed its commitment to achieving the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by 2030, with the National Development Vision 2050 serving as the primary vehicle for delive...

item.title: FIFA Sends French Referees Home Ahead of World Cup Semi-Finals to Avoid Conflicts
item.description: French referees François Letexier and Clément Turpin have been removed from FIFA World Cup 2026 duties after overseeing the controversial Argentina vs Egypt Round of 16 clash. The decision comes as Fr...

item.title: Chadema Official from Geita Faces Legal Proceedings Tomorrow
item.description: A prominent Chadema party member from Geita is set to appear before a magistrate's court tomorrow, marking another development in Tanzania's politically charged Lake Zone region. The case has drawn at...

item.title: Pakistan Backs Saudi Arabia at UNSC, Condemns Houthi Attacks and Calls for Dialogue
item.description: Pakistan reaffirmed its support for Saudi Arabia's security and territorial integrity at a UN Security Council briefing, condemning Houthi attacks on the kingdom. The statement came amid heightened te...

item.title: Understanding Sleep Debt and Recovery: How the Body Handles Short Sleep
item.description: The article explores the concept of 'sleep debt' and clarifies that it's not a financial obligation but a biological process. Sleep pressure, which increases with wakefulness, drives the need for rest...

item.title: Etiquette Tips for Picnics, Travel, and Social Gatherings
item.description: The article provides practical advice on social etiquette and travel considerations. It addresses picnic norms, such as bringing homemade dishes rather than pre-packaged items, and emphasizes sharing ...

item.title: Wife holds husband's legs to prevent him being sucked out of shattered Ryanair plane window at altitude
item.description: A dramatic incident occurred on a Ryanair flight from Thessaloniki in Greece to Memmingen in Germany on Friday when part of the aircraft's engine broke away and smashed through a passenger window at a...

item.title: Stigma and access to care remain barriers to mental health support in South Africa
item.description: The death of Jayden Adams, a 25-year-old footballer, has sparked renewed discussions about mental health in South Africa. Counsellor Shannon Els warns that success in sports, academics, or careers oft...

item.title: Amar Chitra Katha's comic highlights HAL's 86-year aviation journey
item.description: The article discusses the upcoming Amar Chitra Katha (ACK) comic that chronicles Hindustan Aeronautics Limited's (HAL) 86-year journey in Indian aviation. HAL, a Bengaluru-based aircraft manufacturer,...

item.title: Lifestyle Intervention Boosts Cognitive Function in Older Adults
item.description: A two-year structured lifestyle program significantly improved cognitive function in older adults, according to the LatAm-FINGERS trial published in The Lancet. The study involved 1,065 participants a...
